_meta.purpose: Authoritative briefing for HCI documentation and output stabilization. Contains scale provenance, terminology corrections, unit definitions, Stage 1 extensions, and CoDaWork2026 output plan.

system_identity.name: HCI - Higgins Compositional Instrument
system_identity.description: HCI charts where the system is, where it has been, which carriers drove displacement, and which metric directions support future guidance.
system_identity.closing_statement: The instrument reads. The expert decides. The loop stays open.

unit_standard.audio_analogy: Like decibels, HCI uses logarithmic ratio levels. HLR is a natural-log compositional ratio, not an audio amplitude or power unit.

diagonal_sensitivity.name: Diagonal Carrier Steering Sensitivity
diagonal_sensitivity.note: This is NOT the full tensor. It is one diagnostic channel.

dual_coordinate_standard.display_frame: CLR / Higgins Coordinate — one coordinate per carrier, carrier-readable
dual_coordinate_standard.computation_frame: ILR — orthonormal, full-rank, better for tensor algebra and Stage 2/3
dual_coordinate_standard.bridge: h_CLR = B * z_ILR; z_ILR = B_transpose * h_CLR
dual_coordinate_standard.policy: Use CLR for plates, DCDI, and carrier language. Use ILR for orthogonal decomposition and numerical stability.

system_course_plot.name: System Course Plot
system_course_plot.description: Whole-run barycentric plan view showing complete path from first to final observation

documentation_chain.purpose: Main user-facing guide for reading HCI outputs
Define HCI units and scale provenance map
Stage 1 section plates and System Course Plot
Presentation and output package for CoDaWork2026

implementation_note: The arc came full circle. HCI started from audio/log thinking, moved into compositional geometry, built a navigation instrument, and can now return to audio with stronger mathematics. The system can compute its own path.
